channel.title: Haera Wiki
channel.generator: Webpage HTML Export plugin for Obsidian

item.title: Haeran Battlemage Association
item.description:
The Haeran Battlemage Association (HBA) is an association primarily for battle mages in Haera, based in Aethar. It holds a large number of magical artifacts, tomes and items in extra-planar vaults. Further detail is not authorized to be published by the association.There are four ranks for members of the HBA, which are determined by seasonal or annual tests held by higher-rank proctors in their establishments. The ranks are (from highest to lowest): Grand Battlemage Spellwarden Runebinder Initiate The tests for Grand Battlemage are held by renowned Grand Battlemages, and there is a mysterious 'final test' that successful candidates must pass in order to ascend to that role. Otherwise, they are rejected and must remain a Spellwarden. It is possible to be rejected at this stage and pass it in a later test, shown by several mages in past records.Establishments found in:
Aethar
Ego
Faeria
Hero's Hilt

item.title: Aethar
item.description:
Aethar is a floating island in Outer Haera. The Haeran Battlemage Association is based here, and usually only members of the association are allowed to enter, along with Aethar's residents, who are primarily Aasimar. Establishments based in Aethar:
Haeran Battlemage Association

item.title: Hero's Hilt
item.description:
Hero's Hilt is a city located in the south-east end of Split. Its name originates from the belief that a mighty hero's blade created the canyon between the mountains of Split. It primarily consists of halflings and is a major hub for the Adventurer's Guild. Establishments based in Hero's Hilt:
Adventurer's Guild
Haeran Battlemage Association
Hero's Feast

item.title: Faeria
item.description:
Faeria is a coastal city in Western Haera to the north of Lune and west of Raea. It is neighbored by a small forest, and is the largest city in Haera. It is home to humans, halflings, elves and other humanoids. Establishments based in Faeria:
Adventurer's Guild
Government of Faeria
Haeran Battlemage Association
Scholars of Thought
The People's Shield

item.title: Ego
item.description:
Ego is a city in Northern Haera to the south of Ergo. It is home to a wide range of humanoids, primarily humans, gnomes, and some dragonborns. Establishments based in Ego:
Fleetfoot Post
Haeran Battlemage Association
Institute of Natural Sciences
Scholars of Thought
The School of Ego

item.title: Everbrook
item.description:
Everbrook is a group of small towns in Central Haera, bordering Anecdote on its east. Towns: Forager (Northwest) Brookby (Southeast) Establishments situated in Everbrook:
Adventurer's Guild
Fleetfoot Post
Midland Scouts
The People's Shield

item.title: The People's Shield
item.description:
The People's Shield is a guard force deployed across all of Haera, but based in Faeria. It aims to protect towns and cities from monster attacks, and regularly patrols across the continent to answer nearby emergencies if required.
The general public appreciate the Shield's protection, and share positive views for the guard force. The Shield is largely funded by the Government of Faeria, but also earns money via dungeon deployments or cross-continent protection work. Establishment found in:
Abstract
Altrae
Anecdote
Dhor
Dragontail
Ego
Everbrook
Faeria
Gherra
Hero's Hilt
Orhum
Reykja
Thawnia
The Kingdom of Marcell
Whisp

item.title: Seaside Trading Guild
item.description:
"What's yours can be mine, to exact your price divined." Motto of the Seaside Trading Guild The Seaside Trading Guild is a trading and merchant's guild based in Altrae of Eastern Haera. Though Altrae is on the eastern coast of Haera, the guild is very active and prominent in Western Haera, especially the coastal cities Faeria and Gherra. The Seaside Trading Guild is known to operate generously with merchants and traders in cities across Haera, and often rents guildhall spaces to other local guilds, such as local mason, leatherworker, farmer and fishermen guilds. Notable individuals:
Elaine Ridgewater
Gor Schmidt
Hamish Rippletook Establishments found in:
Abermath
Abstract
Altrae
Anecdote
Dhor
Dragontail
Ego
Epiphany
Faeria
Gherra
Hero's Hilt
Nautai
Reykja
Thawnia
The Kingdom of Marcell
Vortex
Whisp

item.title: Fleetfoot Post
item.description:
Fleetfoot Post is a postage and delivery service primarily based in Ego. In collaboration with The School of Ego, Fleetfoot has developed magic-assisted postage systems that can produce temporary portals for pigeon carriers using stored magic (not to be confused with the theory of Stored Magic) acquired from low-energy magic crystals.
Fleetfoot's magical postage system has garnered great attention, and is seen as the best postage service in Haera. Establishments found in:
Abermath
Abstract
Altrae
Dhor
Dragontail
Ego
Epiphany
Everbrook
Faeria
Gherra
Hero's Hilt
Intercept
Nautai
Orhum
Reykja
Spurn
Thawnia
The Kingdom of Marcell
Vortex
Whisp

item.title: Adventurer's Guild
item.description:
The Adventurer's Guild is a far-reaching establishment that supports adventurers across Haera, primarily based in Faeria. It is responsible for contracting adventurers for jobs and quests, and often provides supplies and/or resources for sale to aid them in their adventures.
Hero's Hilt is also an important contributor for the Guild, and is well-endowed with standard adventurer supplies. Establishments found in:
Abstract
Altrae
Anecdote
Crekar
Dhor
Dragontail
Ego
Everbrook
Faeria
Gherra
Hero's Hilt
Intercept
Orhum
Reykja
The Kingdom of Marcell
Vortex
Whisp

item.title: Minor Cities of Haera
item.description:
A list of minor cities in Haera. Minor cities are those with the lesser infrastructure, population or services, with most establishments taking base in nearby Major Cities of Haera.Minor cities:
Abermath
Anecdote
Avador
Crekar
Dhor
Dragontail
Epiphany
Everbrook
Garoa
Krag's Keep
Nautai
Orhum
Reykja
Spurn
Vortex

item.title: Major Cities of Haera
item.description:
A list of major cities in Haera. Major cities are those with the largest infrastructure, population and services, often the 'main' city hub for the nearby region or Minor Cities of Haera.Major cities:
Abstract
Altrae
Ego
Faeria
Gherra
Hero's Hilt
Thawnia
The Kingdom of Marcell
Whisp

item.title: Dhor
item.description: Dhor is a city in Northern Haera. It is located at the northwest corner of the region, and is home to a sizable population.Establishments based in Dhor:

item.title: Northern Cities
item.description:
The Northern Cities consist of various settlements in Northern Haera, including:
Dhor
Ego
Epiphany
Krag's Keep
Orhum
Reykja
Thawnia

item.title: Garoa
item.description: Garoa is a small city in Upper Central Haera, located to the south of Thawnmouth. Establishments based in Garoa:

item.title: Upper Central Haera
item.description:
Upper Central Haera is a region of cold plains and forests in the northern center of Haera, consisting of:
Abermath
Conduit
Garoa
Intercept
Northwind
Sandspine
Thawnmouth
The Kingdom of Marcell
Whisp

item.title: Northern Mountains
item.description:
The Northern Mountains consist of various mountain ranges in the north of Haera, including:
Ascent
Ergo
Exha
Krag
Schmine
Snowspine
Soarre

item.title: Ascent
item.description: Ascent is a mountain range in the Frostlands. Its largest mountain ascends into the clouds, earning its name.

item.title: Frostlands
item.description: The Frostlands is a large pains with numerous mountain ranges in Northern Haera. Avador and Ascent lies within the Frostlands.

item.title: Thawnmouth
item.description: Thawnmouth is a set of mountains surrounding a lake in Upper Central Haera. Thawnia lies in its north.

item.title: Avador
item.description: Avador is a mountain city in Northern Haera, specifically, the Frostlands. Establishments based in Avador:

item.title: Northern Haera
item.description: Northern Haera is a region of snowy plains, forests and mountains in the north of Haera, consisting of the Northern Mountains, Northern Cities and the Frostlands.

item.title: Regions of Haera
item.description:
A list of large regions in Haera:
Northern Haera
Upper Central Haera
Eastern Haera
Central Haera
Western Haera
Southern Haera
Outer Haera

item.title: Western Haera
item.description:
Western Haera is a region of vast plains, small forests with a small selection of mountain ranges, located in the west of Haera. It consists of:
Crekar
Drakehidt
Faeria
Gherra
Landspine
Lune
Raea
Windswept Plains

item.title: Crekar
item.description: Crekar is a small city in Western Haera. It hosts a small population, with mountains to its north and south.Establishments based in Crekar:

item.title: The Warring Times
item.description:
The Warring Times was a period of strife and chaos in the History of Haera, succeeding the magnificence of The Classical Era. Numerous kingdom leaders and cities were manipulated or pushed into battle or war across Haera, while monster populations rose due to the decline in emergence of legendary heroes.Warriors and mages during this period specialized heavily in battle and combat, shaping the development and production of magical items or mechanical creations towards more malevolent causes. Though rare, extremely powerful individuals hailing during these times were battle-hardened, savage, and had uncontested strength in their craft.
The Modern Era succeeds this era.

item.title: The Classical Era
item.description:
The Classical Era succeeds The Forgotten Times in the History of Haera, and was a largely stable era where fragmented settlements and groups across Haera began interacting and trading under benign intentions.
Many legendary heroes emerged during this era with mystical weapons and artefacts, fending off demonic beasts, region-level threats and monstrous aberrations with profound might. Many kingdoms and cities flourished and grew under their protection, forming the core foundation of The Modern Era we now know today.
The Forgotten Times precedes this era.

item.title: Dilor Griffin
item.description: Dilor Griffin is the Dwarf leader of the College of Mechanical Studies. Throughout his years at the College, he has crafted numerous outstanding and unique mechanical devices and weaponry, earning his grand reputation in the College and eventually becoming its leader.

item.title: Winery's Whisp
item.description:
Winery's Whisp is a brewery located in Whisp. Though its products are spread far and wide across Haera, the establishment itself remains situated in Whisp. Its founding family, the Borf Family, founded and maintained the core vinery of the company across The Classical Era up till The Modern Era.Establishments found in:
Whisp

item.title: Borf Family
item.description: The Borf Family is a family of dwarves in Whisp who own, manage and run Winery's Whisp. It has extensive trade connections with the Seaside Trading Guild and the Adventurer's Guild, supplying wines and brews for numerous eras.

item.title: Reformed Seafarers
item.description:
The Reformed Seafarers is a sea exploration organization based in Gherra. It was founded and currently led by a majority of reformed pirates and sea looters, with some hailing from distant lands. They spearhead ocean exploration and expeditions, and have had numerous bountiful adventures, bringing back remarkable artefacts, treasure or research material. Establishments found in:
Gherra

item.title: Krag's Keep
item.description: Krag's Keep is a set of small towns in Northern Haera, located in the south of Krag. It is founded by saints and churches in The Classical Era, originally worshipping and teaching the mysterious and fantastical phenomenon of Krag.Establishments based in Krag's Keep:

item.title: Nautai
item.description: Intercept is a small coastal city in Eastern Haera, to the south of Abstract. It focuses mostly on fishing, but also boasts several exotic fruit and bird species, highly coveted by merchants and traders. Establishments based in Abstract:

item.title: Intercept
item.description: Intercept is a small city in Upper Central Haera, to the north of The Kingdom of Marcell. Establishments based in Intercept:

item.title: Epiphany
item.description: Epiphany is a small city in Northern Haera, to the north of Krag. Its calm, cold and windy weather has attracted many monks across Haera to meditate and train there, and is believed to have the capability of awakening its namesake for those who are truly dedicated to their beliefs and training.Establishments based in Epiphany:

item.title: Gherra
item.description:
Gherra is a coastal city in Western Haera, to the north of Faeria. It is a prominent seafaring region, and often sends expeditions into the sea in search of distant lands, islands or treasures. Establishments based in Gherra:
Reformed Seafarers

item.title: Abermath
item.description: Abermath is a small city in Upper Central Haera. It is a coastal settlement that primarily earns its keep via fishing and seafood trade. Establishments based in Abermath:

item.title: Kinsguard
item.description:
The Kinsguard (not Kingsguard) is a guard force primarily trained and based in The Kingdom of Marcell. It was first introduced by Queen Amelia Lukeheart during The Classical Era.
Though not strictly bound to The Kingdom of Marcell, the Kinsguard usually exclusively operates to protect the citizens of Marcell from monstrous threats. Seldomly, the Kinsguard may be deployed to protect important carriages as they journey across Haera. They are also enlisted to protect Abermath and Intercept.Establishments found in:
Abermath
Intercept
The Kingdom of Marcell

item.title: Eastern Haera
item.description:
Eastern Haera is a region of temperate forests, plains, and a rainforest in the east of Haera. A large desert lies in its north.It consists of:
Abstract
Altrae
Churn
Merphos
Nautai
Renavoir

item.title: Whisp
item.description:
Whisp is a city in Upper Central Haera to the west of Conduit. Though not the biggest of cities within Haera, it is one of the only active trading cities with Thawnia. Establishments based in Whisp:
Winery's Whisp

item.title: Churn
item.description: Churn is a desert region mostly surrounded by mountains in the north of Eastern Haera, consisting of the cities Spurn and Vortex.

item.title: Establishments of Haera
item.description:
A list of establishments in Haera:
Adventurer's Guild
Fleetfoot Post
Government of Faeria
Hero's Feast
House of Alonzo
Kinsguard
Midland Scouts
Seaside Trading Guild
The People's Shield A list of academic schools/establishments of Haera:
Academic Schools of Haera

item.title: Midland Scouts
item.description:
The Midland Scouts are a small guard and scout force primarily deployed in Anecdote and Everbrook. They also have an established station in The Kingdom of Marcell, though the Kinsguard is much more prevalent there.Establishments found in:
Anecdote
Everbrook
The Kingdom of Marcell

item.title: Anecdote
item.description:
Anecdote is a city in Central Haera, bordering Everbrook to its west. Establishments based in Anecdote:
Midland Scouts

item.title: Forsaken Perch
item.description: The Forsaken Perch is an impressive mountain range in Southern Haera boasting five extremely tall peaks, arranged in the shape of a '^'. Many Monks and Clerics have come to these mountains for training or worship, as its presence is believed to be enlightening or celestial by many.Small towns are built near the mountains as a result, and tourism is an advancing business for those wishing to gaze upon the mountains more closely.

item.title: Greyhorde
item.description:
Greyhorde is a vast mountain range in Southern Haera. It is home to many dwarves and Dragonborn, with extensive underground communities and colonies built beneath its surface.
Due to the nature of such underground communities, documentation and research from outside sources in Haera are strictly prohibited, and thus academic schools have failed to provide further information about the mountains and its inhabitants.

item.title: Remnant
item.description: Remnant is a small mountain range in the north of Southern Haera. It is rumored that extensive cave systems lie under it, but expeditions have not successfully identified a clear path down into it.

item.title: Eror
item.description: Eror is a small dead forest in Southern Haera, where all life has been extinguished due to some unknown past event, speculated to have occurred long ago during The Forgotten Times.Attempts to restore life in the region have been made, but all of them failed. It remains a barren wasteland of skeletal trees and fossils.

item.title: Egia Desert
item.description: The Egia Desert is a large desert in Southern Haera. It is an extremely dry region, and temperature changes from day to night are very drastic.

item.title: Blighted Lands
item.description:
The Blighted Lands is a corrupted forest in Southern Haera. It is devoid of natural life, and the region is speculated to have experienced cross-plane bleed from the Shadowfell. A bizarre, inky black sky hovers over the region, with decay and death encompassing its woods.
Many academic schools have discouraged expeditions to the Blighted Lands, with the Institute of Natural Sciences being the sole academic school that obtained one of the only threads of information known about the region: it hosts some form of life.

item.title: Obsidian Peaks
item.description: The Obsidian Peaks are an array of violent volcanic mountains in Outer Haera, with remarkable height and power. As if some portraying an animal's defensive mechanism, attempts to reach and land on the Peaks have always failed as such expeditions are always met with sudden, unpredictable volcanic activity, forcing them to return to mainland Haera.

item.title: Conduit Travel Theory
item.description:
Conduit Travel Theory (CTT) is a theory based on the magical energy lingering around the mountains of Conduit. Through years of study and research, schools and magic-related groups have arrived to this theory.CTT suggests that the lingering magical energy can be manipulated and 'bound' to certain planes, and this bond can be enhanced with the magical energy of a user. Warlocks have been extensively invited to Conduit for the testing of this theory, due to their inter-planar connections with their patrons, but results are neither confirming nor denying the theory, as there is currently no method to determine whether the bond has been formed.The primary contributors to CTT are:
Tazmir Avhor
The School of Ego
The Circle of Ley Lines
Some may argue that Chromatia could fit into CTT, but this claim is widely disagreed with.

item.title: Chromatia
item.description:
Chromatia is a ring of colorful islands in Outer Haera. It is established by The Circle of Ley Lines and the Institute of Natural Sciences that the islands emit magic in similar ways to Conduit.
However, it is widely agreed that Chromatia does not fall into Conduit Travel Theory, as calibrations and experiments regarding inter-planar travel are inconsistent and do not suggest any relation to the travel method described by the theory.

item.title: Blood Frosts
item.description:
"If it wasn't for those magical particles, we'd have explored the whole island millennia ago." Professor Forbur Wilkinberry of The School of Ego,
The Blood Frosts is a snowy island in Outer Haera that consists of a large mountain range and purplish-red tints in the snow. The island frequently emits dangerous magical particles that are harmful to any who approach, thus halting research and investigation efforts.

item.title: Intrigue
item.description: Intrigue is a mysterious island in Outer Haera. It boasts an odd geological appearance, with towering mountains and peaks surrounded by quaint palm trees. Generations of monks and philosophers have travelled to Intrigue to seek inner peace or training, similar to the Forsaken Perch.Its exotic palm trees produce various species of interesting fruit, often sold for very high prices due to its remoteness and exquisite taste.

item.title: Vortex
item.description: Spurn is a city in the east of Churn. It is an exotic destination for most populations of Haera, and is home to numerous exotic and unique species. Establishments based in Vortex:

item.title: Spurn
item.description: Spurn is a city in the north of Churn. It is somewhat near the ocean but not a coastal city. Establishments based in Spurn:

item.title: index
item.description: Welcome to the landing page of the Haera wiki!Get started by visiting Haera, or navigate through the wiki via the sidebar.

item.title: Scholars of Thought
item.description:
The Scholars of Thought are a collection and school of philosophers, historians and theorists well known throughout Haera. The Scholars have extensive reach across the continent, with primary bases in Ego, Faeria and Altrae. Notable individuals:
Queen Masja Alonzo Establishments found in:
Abstract
Altrae
Anecdote
Ego
Faeria
Reykja
The Kingdom of Marcell

item.title: The School of Ego
item.description:
The School of Ego is an established college primarily for wizards and sorcerers, where they work together to formulate magical apparatus, spells and techniques. They are based in the city of Ego in Northern Haera.Notable individuals:
Professor Forbur Wilkinberry
Professor Seradine Blackwood Establishments found in:
Altrae
Ego
Faeria
Hero's Hilt
Reykja
Thawnia

item.title: Institute of Natural Sciences
item.description:
The Institute of Natural Sciences is a well-respected institute that studies and researches magical, non-magical, cursed and benign nature. It is primarily based in Ego, and is one of the only establishments allowed within Thawnia. Notable individuals:
Professor Kahl Czekh Establishments found in:
Ego
Faeria
Thawnia

item.title: College of Mechanical Studies
item.description:
The College of Mechanical Studies is a school for mechanics and tinkerers in Haera. Its current leader is Dilor Griffin. They are based in the city Dragontail. Notable individuals:
Dilor Griffin Establishments found in:
Abstract
Dragontail
Ego
Faeria

item.title: Haera
item.description:
Haera is a continent in the Material Plane, home to a diverse range of humanoids, such as humans or elves. Regions of Haera:
Regions of Haera Cities in Haera:
Major Cities of Haera
Minor Cities of Haera Establishments in Haera:
Establishments of Haera Academic schools in Haera:
Academic Schools of Haera

item.title: Academic Schools of Haera
item.description:
A list of academic schools, establishments and organizations in Haera:
College of Mechanical Studies
Institute of Natural Sciences
Scholars of Thought
The Circle of Ley Lines
The School of Ego

item.title: House of Alonzo
item.description:
The House of Alonzo is a prevalent House in Abstract, but despite its ties to the Abstract monarchy, is a well-established and respected organization that researches and develops mechanical devices, with members from the Scholars of Thought, and primarily Dwarf, Halfling, Human and Aasimar members.
The House also supervises the College of Mechanical Studies, but its leader Dilor Griffin retains ultimate management of the College. Notable members:
Queen Masja Alonzo
Juan Alonzo (deceased) Establishments found in:
Abstract

item.title: Drakehidt
item.description:
Drakehidt (pronounced Drake-height) is a peculiar mountain range in Western Haera that largely resembles dragon scales.
The Circle of Ley Lines has previously investigated the mountains with Ley Lines, but were inconclusive on whether these scales originated from the ruins of a long-forgotten dragon, or some other beast.

item.title: Exha
item.description: Exha is a small mountain range in Northern Haera. It is infamous for being home to many dangerous monsters and beasts, as it hosts a vast array of hidden and largely unexplored cave systems within.

item.title: Lune
item.description:
Lune is an inactive volcano in Western Haera accompanied by a small mountain range, with Raea to its north and the Windswept Plains to its east.
Though the volcano has not been active since The Warring Times, it is speculated by the Institute of Natural Sciences and The Circle of Ley Lines that the volcano may still erupt under unknown circumstances.

item.title: Raea
item.description:
Raea is a small mountain range in Western Haera, with Faeria to the east and Lune to the south.
Raea is a well-known hot springs attraction. Though the mountains make it infeasible to construct buildings, guided hikes and paths have been laid out to support tourism and visits from all over Haera.
The Institute of Natural Sciences, The Circle of Ley Lines and The School of Ego have confirmed with empirical evidence that the hot spring waters originating from Raea's geothermal sources produce subtle and refreshing healing qualities.

item.title: Windswept Plains
item.description: The Windswept Plains are a large region of grassy plains in Western Haera, with Raea and Lune to the west.

item.title: Tiefling
item.description: Tieflings are humanoids who have fiendish ancestry. They have a life span of around 750 years, and don't sleep.They can be of medium (4-7 ft) or small size (3-4 ft), with a speed of 30 feet.Fiendish Legacies: Abyssal Chthonic Infernal

item.title: Orc
item.description: Orcs are tall and broad humanoids with high endurance and determination.They can be of medium size (6-7 ft), with a speed of 30 feet.

item.title: Human
item.description: Humans are diverse, ambitious and resourceful humanoids.They can be of medium (4-7 ft) or small size (2-4 ft), with a speed of 30 feet.

item.title: Halfling
item.description: Halflings are humanoids who are very lucky. They have a life span of around 150 years.They can be of small size (2-3 ft), with a speed of 30 feet.

item.title: Goliath
item.description: Goliaths are humanoids who are descendants of giants. They can be of medium size (7-8 ft), with a speed of 35 feet.

item.title: Gnome
item.description: Gnomes are clever humanoids who have pointed ears and big eyes. They have a life span of around 425 years.They can be of small size (3-4 ft), with a speed of 30 feet.

item.title: Elf
item.description: Elves are humanoids that may have been shaped by their environments over the years. They have a life span of around 750 years, and don't sleep.They can be of medium size (5-6 ft), with a speed of 30 feet (35 feet for wood elves).Elven Lineages: Drow High Elf Wood Elf

item.title: Dwarf
item.description: Dwarves are resilient humanoids who have an affinity for stone, metal and living underground. They have a life span of around 350 years.They can be of medium size (4-5 ft), with a speed of 30 feet.

item.title: Dragonborn
item.description: Dragonborn are humanoids that look like wingless, bipedal dragons, with horns on their heads. Their colorations and features are reminiscent of their draconic ancestors.They can be of medium size (5-7 ft), with a speed of 30 feet.Draconic Ancestors: Black Blue Brass Bronze Copper

item.title: Aasimar
item.description: Aasimar are humanoids who carry a spark of the Upper Planes within their souls. They can be descended from angelic beings or infused with Celestial power, fanning that spark to bring light, healing and heavenly fury.They can be of medium (4-7ft) or small size (2-4ft), with a speed of 30 feet.

item.title: Tazmir Avhor
item.description:
Tazmir Avhor is a Tiefling Warlock who has a pact with a Fiend within the Nine Hells. Despite her infernal origins, Avhor has participated in many research studies regarding pact magic, inter-planar energy transfer and inter-planar travel.
Most notably, Avhor was a major contributor to the Conduit Travel Theory, being the most enthusiastic participant who joined the research team to discern potential results at Conduit.

item.title: The Circle of Ley Lines
item.description:
The Circle of Ley Lines is a small, journeying druidic circle of scholars that aim to define the world via Ley Lines. Though whimsical, traces of the Circle have been observed in numerous of Haera's regions exhibiting more interesting natural phenomenon, such as Canvas and Conduit.
The Institute of Natural Sciences have attempted to locate the Circle after hearing rumors that they originated from Canvas, but has failed to reach them thus far.
The Circle of Ley Lines have contributed to the Conduit Travel Theory, one of the few times the Circle has remained in a specific city for more than a week.

item.title: Krag
item.description:
"Truly spectacular. There is potential in these mountains for preserving life in an unorthodox manner." Professor Kahl Czekh, Institute of Natural Sciences
Krag is a small set of mountains in Northern Haera that exhibits strange ecological behavior, with deciduous trees remaining green in a small region around the mountains, despite the harsh cold weather all year long.
Researchers and scholars across Northern Haera have attempted to discern the cause and replicate the effects of Krag, but manually-placed nature do not exhibit the same effects as the pre-existing nature growing on the mountain. It is thought that this unbelievable nature is produced over many, many years of evolution under the effects of the mountain, and if removed, lose its connection to its powers completely.

item.title: Canvas
item.description:
"Home to plenty of magical kin, magical forests and a magical lake. Truly a remarkable geographical sight." Prof. Forbur Wilkinberry, The School of Ego
Canvas is a very large forest in Central Haera, bordering Stockold, Sandspine and Split. It is home to many elves, druids and other fantastical creatures.Canvas revolves around an enormous lake which is said to provide healing properties if processed correctly with the correct materials.
The Institute of Natural Sciences has sent numerous expeditions to Canvas, but often its participants find themselves charmed and removed from the forest, perhaps due to its inhabitants rejecting such rigorous research activity taking place.

item.title: Kahl Czekh
item.description: Kahl Czech is a renowned Dragonborn professor and instructor at the Institute of Natural Sciences. He is the pioneer in leading developments and projects in natural sciences across Haera, and was responsible for Thawnia's acceptance of the Institute taking base within it.

item.title: Southern Haera
item.description:
Southern Haera is a region largely consisting of deserts, mountains and life-less lands, in the south of Haera.It consists of:
Blighted Lands
Egia Desert
Eror
Forsaken Perch
Greyhorde
Remnant

item.title: Humanoid
item.description:
Humanoids are a creature type, ranging from small sizes (2-4 feet tall) to medium (4-8 feet tall). Here are some humanoids in the land of Haera:
Aasimar
Dragonborn
Dwarf
Elf
Gnome
Goliath
Halfling
Human
Orc
Tiefling

item.title: Government of Faeria
item.description:
Faeria is run by the Government of Faeria. It was first introduced after The Warring Times where the acting monarch, Queen Farlyn Mysth descended the throne to aid war efforts.
In The Modern Era, the government manages establishments across the city, as well as funding The People's Shield and the Adventurer's Guild.Establishments found in:
Faeria

item.title: Reykja
item.description: Reykja is a city in Northern Haera. It is located to the northeast of Soarre and west of Orhum.Establishments based in Reykja:

item.title: Abstract
item.description:
Abstract is a kingdom consisting of smaller towns and cities in Eastern Haera that is located near Renavoir. It is home to many humans, halflings, and other humanoids, and one of the last regions on Haera that is still run by a monarch.
The current monarch is Queen Masja Alonzo, who has reigned for the past three years since the death of her brother Juan Alonzo. Monarchs:
Queen Masja Alonzo (current, The Modern Era)
Juan Alonzo (The Modern Era) Establishments based in Abstract:
House of Alonzo

item.title: Orhum
item.description: Orhum is a city in Northern Haera. It is located to the north of Soarre and northwest of Reykja.Establishments based in Orhum:

item.title: Dragontail
item.description: Dragontail is a city in Central Haera to the south of Landspine. Establishments based in Dragontail:

item.title: Central Haera
item.description:
Central Haera is a region of lush, green forests accompanied by large hills, mountain ranges, and a marsh, located in the center of Haera.It consists of:
Anecdote
Canvas
Dragontail
Everbrook
Landspine
Split
Stockold

item.title: King Arthur Lukeheart II
item.description:
"Soon, a great peril will fall upon the Kingdom. Evil incarnate, in the form of a devastating dragon will strike Marcell. It is by defeating this malignant creature that my son will be crowned King." Prophecy by Arthur Lukeheart three years before the incident.
King Arthur Lukeheart II is the current Human king of The Kingdom of Marcell. He is a valiant warrior in battle, having conquered many foes in regional battles mostly around Upper Central Haera. His late father, Arthur Lukeheart deemed his child will be destined for kingship after slaying a mighty dragon, which ultimately occurred at the cost of several lives, including that of his father.
item.guid: Characters/Non Playable Characters/Monarchs/King Arthur Lukeheart II.md

item.title: Juan Alonzo
item.description:
"He radiated celestial light to pave the path for others throughout his orbit. May he now rest in peace." Queen Masja Alonzo in the public funeral of Juan Alonzo.
Juan Alonzo was the Aasimar king of Abstract and died at age 54 during a battle near Landspine. In life, Alonzo was a strong ruler who was fair and just. He was a powerful Paladin, but lacked inventive creativity, hence did not participate in the proceedings of the House of Alonzo.

item.title: Renavoir
item.description: Renavoir is a rainforest situated in the south of Eastern Haera. It is home to many exotic animals, species and fruit. Its density and high humidity has made it difficult for cartographers to map out the region properly over the years, and many parts of it remain unexplored.Many adventurers have attempted to find hidden treasure in Renavoir, though thus far none have returned successful, or returned at all.

item.title: Hero's Feast
item.description:
Hero's Feast is a Halfling tavern chain primarily based in Hero's Hilt, founded during The Classical Era by Chardine Suave. Signature dishes: Grilled giant chicken's leg and titan potato soup Deluxe steak and titan potato fries Cheesy lobster and prawn (coastal establishments only) Hero's Feast (Hero's Hilt only) salads procured from regional vegetables titan potato soup multiple species of bird and land animals served in various techniques: grilled, fried, baked with titan potato fries
exotic fruits from Renavoir and Intrigue. Hero's Feast locations:
Hero's Hilt
Abstract
Altrae
Faeria
The Kingdom of Marcell

item.title: Chardine Suave
item.description: Chardine Suave is a Halfling who founded Hero's Feast in Hero's Hilt. He was a wonderful chef whose spectacular culinary expertise was praised for many years, even after his passing.

item.title: Stored Magic
item.description:
"A breakthrough in Stored Magic could vastly improve strength and power of guards and armies, greatly heightening their performance against monstrous threats." Professor Forbur Wilkinberry.
Stored magic is a magical theory by Forbur Wilkinberry and Seradine Blackwood that attunement to magic items requires a "magically-tangible" manifestation of the user's soul, thus aligning one's soul to the corresponding item.
Wilkinberry and Blackwood theorised that through capturing and replicating behaviors of a user's soul, it may be possible to duplicate the effect of attunement with magical apparatus, potentially allowing for rapid attunement switching and magic item use in combat scenarios. However, the theory is currently facing opposition in The School of Ego as success in this theory could become very dangerous in the wrong hands.

item.title: Queen Masja Alonzo
item.description:
Queen Masja Alonzo is the current Aasimar monarch of Abstract. Before she became Queen, she was a notable inventor in the House of Alonzo, developing many trinkets and devices helpful to the general public.
She was a prevalent council member of the Scholars of Thought, and had to step down after the passing of her brother Juan Alonzo three years ago. She is an amiable leader and members of Abstract are content with her ruling.

item.title: Northwind
item.description: Northwind is a region within Upper Central Haera, covering a significant portion of the area's cold forests. It contains numerous small settlements, towns and minor cities.

item.title: History of Haera
item.description:
The history of Haera is largely defined by four eras, in chronological order: The Forgotten Times
The Classical Era
The Warring Times
The Modern Era

item.title: The Forgotten Times
item.description:
The Forgotten Times is the earliest era in the History of Haera, and only fragmented knowledge of this era is known, due to extensive monster activity in many regions, making it difficult for regions of Haera to communicate with each other. It is thought that only high-ranking families and clans have access to ancestral records referencing potential events and recollections of this era.
Though the Scholars of Thought have repeatedly attempted to acquire information about The Forgotten Times, every known family or clan have turned them down, potentially due to some of the contents presented in such records.
The Classical Era succeeds this era.

item.title: Altrae
item.description:
Altrae is a city in Eastern Haera that is located near Merphos. It is a coastal city, home to many humans, halflings, and other humanoids. Establishments based in Altrae:
Seaside Trading Guild
Scholars of Thought

item.title: Outer Haera
item.description:
Outer Haera consists of locations and regions disconnected from the main continent of Haera.It consists of:
Aethar
Blood Frosts
Chromatia
Intrigue
Obsidian Peaks

item.title: Split
item.description:
Split is a small mountain range in Central Haera with a river that flows directly through the mountains.
It is rumored that in The Forgotten Times, a powerful demi-god or hero slashed through the mountain range with a powerful greatsword during a conflict.

item.title: Landspine
item.description: Landspine is a vast multi-directional mountain range that borders Western Haera, Central Haera and the mountains of Drakehidt.

item.title: The Kingdom of Marcell
item.description:
The Kingdom of Marcell consists of numerous cities in Upper Central Haera to the west of Sandspine. It is one of the last regions on Haera that is still run by a monarch.
The current monarch is King Arthur Lukeheart II, who has participated in numerous regional battles in the past. Monarchs:
King Arthur Lukeheart II (current, The Modern Era)
Arthur Lukeheart (The Modern Era)
Amelia Lukeheart (The Classical Era)
Vasir Lukeheart (The Classical Era) Establishments based in The Kingdom of Marcell:
Kinsguard

item.title: Vasir Lukeheart
item.description: Vasir Lukeheart was a Human king of The Kingdom of Marcell during The Classical Era. He was assassinated at age 47 during his reign after he caused numerous incidents that outraged his people and plummeted the reputation of the Kinsguard, who he bribed and used to selectively protect particular cities within Marcell, leaving the rest to fend for themselves against devastating attacks and raids.

item.title: Amelia Lukeheart
item.description:
Amelia Lukeheart was a prominent and famed Human Queen of The Kingdom of Marcell during The Classical Era. Amelia passed peacefully at the age of 66, handing the crown to her son Vasir.
She introduced the Kinsguard to Marcell, and was the first Queen of Marcell since The Forgotten Times.

item.title: Conduit
item.description:
Conduit is a group of mountains that emerge from a vast lake towards the west of Upper Central Haera.
Schools of wizardry, sorcery, churches and druids have studied Conduit for many years, and it is widely theorised that the mountains are host to very high magical energy, and if correct calibration and an inter-planar connection is made, it may be possible to harness the energy of Conduit to travel to other planes. See more in Conduit Travel Theory.

item.title: Arthur Lukeheart
item.description: Arthur Lukeheart (not to be confused with King Arthur Lukeheart II) was the Human king of The Kingdom of Marcell and died at age 72, being defeated by an evil dragon later slain by his son, King Arthur Lukeheart II. In life, Lukeheart occasionally had bursts of prophetic vision, which has influenced his ruling over the course of his kingship. Citizens were generally amiable towards his use of his strange power, and did not attempt to thwart his reign as he was a wise king whose tactical and strategic knowledge was greatly valued in battle, and was considerably generous to the common people.

item.title: Hamish Rippletook
item.description:
Hamish Rippletook is the acting director of the Seaside Trading Guild Guildhall based in Faeria. He is a Gnome. Though Faeria is on the opposite side of Haera to Altrae, Rippletook is a reliable ally of Elaine Ridgewater since before she became the leader of the guild.
Rippletook is often seen performing trade in the public market, protected by bodyguards of The People's Shield.
item.guid: Characters/Non Playable Characters/People in Power/Hamish Rippletook.md

item.title: Gor Schmidt
item.description: Gor Schmidt is the second-in-command of the Seaside Trading Guild. He is a Human based in Altrae. Though Schmidt works under Elaine Ridgewater, he is seen more often in public areas than her, taking over minor dealings in her place or investigating and recruiting incoming merchants.Schmidt is often seen as a bodyguard for Ridgewater in important transactions, and it is well-known that he was previously an adventurer as a Rogue.
item.guid: Characters/Non Playable Characters/People in Power/Gor Schmidt.md

item.title: Elaine Ridgewater
item.description:
Elaine Ridgewater is the current owner and leader of the Seaside Trading Guild. She is a Halfling based in Altrae and is rarely seen outside of the Seaside Trading Guildhall. It is said that she orchestrates some of the most influential and important trades in the city of Altrae, and may be involved in numerous dealings in nearby regions.
Her second-in-command is Gor Schmidt, who can often be seen alongside her during big trade discussions and transactions.
item.guid: Characters/Non Playable Characters/People in Power/Elaine Ridgewater.md

item.title: Thawnia
item.description: Thawnia is a city in Northern Haera. It is home to Dragonborns, ...Establishments based in Thawnia:

item.title: Stockold
item.description: Stockold is a mountain range in Central Haera that is located near Canvas.

item.title: Seradine Blackwood
item.description: Seradine Blackwood is the wife of Forbur Wilkinberry, and a highly renowned Human Sorcerer in the The School of Ego. She pioneered the development and theory of Stored Magic with her husband, paving the path forwards to attunement-less magic item use.

item.title: Forbur Wilkinberry
item.description: Forbur Wilkinberry is a Gnome professor in The School of Ego who is regarded as one of the greatest wizard academics of The Modern Era, having invented numerous items that store and contain magic, and pioneered the development and theory of Stored Magic along with his wife Seradine Blackwood.

item.title: The Modern Era
item.description: The Modern Era is the current era in which Haera lies. It succeeds The Warring Times in the History of Haera.

item.title: Ergo
item.description:
Ergo is part of the Northern Mountains in Haera, stretching across the north of the continent. It lies to the east of Soarre and to the north of Snowspine.
It is speculated by the The School of Ego that the highest point in Ergo leads to the Upper Planes, or contains great compatibility for inter-planar travel.

item.title: Merphos
item.description: Merphos is a vast forest in Eastern Haera home to many elves, druids, ....

item.title: Sandspine
item.description: Sandspine is a vast mountain range that encompasses part of Churn and borders Merphos.

item.title: Snowspine
item.description: Snowspine is part of the Northern Mountains in Haera, stretching across the north of the continent. It borders Ergo to the north and Schmine to the southwest.

item.title: Soarre
item.description: Soarre is part of the Northern Mountains in Haera, stretching across the north of the continent. It lies to the west of Ergo and to the north of Schmine.

item.title: Schmine
item.description: Schmine is part of the Northern Mountains in Haera, stretching across the central north of the continent. It lies on the border of Northern Haera and Upper Central Haera.

item.title: Inner Planes
item.description:
The Inner Planes are Planes consisting of:
Elemental Plane of Air
Elemental Plane of Earth
Elemental Plane of Fire
Elemental Plane of Water
Elemental Chaos
Para-Elemental Planes

item.title: Ethereal Plane
item.description: The Ethereal Plane is a Plane in the Transitive Planes, ...

item.title: Astral Plane
item.description: The Astral Plane is a Plane in the Transitive Planes, ...

item.title: Transitive Planes
item.description:
Transitive planes serve as paths between planes, including: Astral Plane
Ethereal Plane

item.title: Shadowfell
item.description: The Shadowfell is a Plane in The Material Realms, home to grim wastelands, haunted ruins and the Domains of Dread.

item.title: Material Plane
item.description:
The material plane is a Plane in the The Material Realms, home to many humanoids, such as humans and elves.
One notable continent in the material plane is Haera.

item.title: Feywild
item.description: The Feywild is a Plane in The Material Realms, home to...

item.title: The Material Realms
item.description:
The Material Realms consist of Planes including:
Material Plane
Feywild
Shadowfell

item.title: Plane
item.description:
A plane of existence. Planes include: The Material Realms
Transitive Planes
Inner Planes
Outer Planes

item.title: Lower Planes
item.description:
The lower planes are a subset of the Outer Planes consisting of:
Abyss
Acheron
Carceri
Gehenna
Hades
Nine Hells
Pandemonium

item.title: Outer Planes
item.description:
The Outer Planes are Planes consisting of:
Abyss
Acheron
Arborea
Arcadia
Beastlands
Bytopia
Carceri
Elysium
Gehenna
Hades
Limbo
Mechanus
Mount Celestia
Nine Hells
Outlands
Pandemonium
Ysgard Two subsets of the Outer Planes include:
Lower Planes
Upper Planes

item.title: Upper Planes
item.description:
The Upper Planes are a subset of the Outer Planes consisting of:
Arborea
Arcadia
Beastlands
Bytopia
Elysium
Mount Celestia
Ysgard
